IN THE COURT OF APPEAL OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT DIVISION SIX PG INN, INC., 2d Civil No. B248589 (Super. Ct. No. 56-2012-00428313- Plaintiff and Respondent, CU-DF-VTA) (Ventura County) v. CHRIS GATWARD et al., Defendants and Appellants. Chris Gatward (Gatward) and M3 Environmental Consulnting, Inc. (M3), the defendants and appellants in this case appeal from a judgment entered after a bench trial on their breach of contract claim against plaintiff PG Inn, Inc., an LLC owned by Gatward's brother David Gatward. The central issue presented is whether the parties' written agreement provided that M3 was entitled to its fee for providing consulting services under all circumstances. The trial court found in favor of PG Inn and ordered that M3 recover nothing from PG Inn on this breach of contract claim, finding there were no genuine issues of material fact as to Gatward's obligations or entitlements. We agree with the trial court regarding both the factual issue and its legal conclusion: under California law governing contracts, a party is not entitled to payment for services rendered if that party breached

But that’s not the only perspective. Many believe government is meant to be an extension of us, We the People — intended to not dictate and control, but be subordinate to our demands. While no institution is or will ever be perfect, none can ultimately provide real democracy and no public official is completely representative of his/her constituents, our governments (from the local to the national — including executives, legislatures and judges) are certainly worth having — if for no other reason than these public structures serve as a counterweight to ever-growing power of private structures. These specifically are for profit corporations. This perspective asserts the reason for a major part of government corruption is that private/corporate institutions have become so powerful that they have profoundly influenced public/government institutions. This influence has resulted in the perversion of laws, rules and regulations (i.e. spending, tax and monetary policies, as well as regulations and court decisions) that unduly favor corporations and the super wealthy — the 2 largest entities contributing/investing in political campaigns. Government corruption, therefore, is a result of government capture — to benefit a few at the expense of the many. The increasing belief that governments are corrupt coincides with the increasing political influence of corporations and the super wealthy. The solution is not per se less government (though there are certainly examples where government interference has became antidemocratic). It’s reducing the ability of corporations and the super wealthy to influence governments. There is no one antidote to reduce the influence of the super wealthy and corporations. texts.full_text: hich may include some degree of compromise), their character would still be seen as having bad traits because it's easier for viewers than taking time out think beyond the surface level. The reason why this is wrong lies not just in simplicity, but also due to its tendency leading towards a self-fulfilling prophecy—the more we are fed simple stories and caricatures about certain characters or political groups (especially those who oppose capitalism), it makes us less able for critical thinking. In the end it might make people even harder at understanding these complexities that come from different views on issues. To illustrate this point further, let's take an example of someone being portrayed as a capitalist-supporting politician — one would have to ask themselves: why is he/she like? What led him/her there in their journey through life? By having more varied and realistic portrayals (rather than simple black-and-white characters), it allows the audience not only see what's going on, but also understand better behind each person or group of people. The result would be that we'll all become less polarised because these stories will reflect our world with a greater degree accuracy. I truly hope to one day witness more TV thrillers and movies which do this properly—they don't just tell us about political issues, but actually engage viewers' minds in the process too (something I believe should be at heart of any story telling). However even if it's going slow on achieving its goal for now – we can still hope that by making our voices heard more people will begin to see and understand what needs doing. So as long this remains a significant issue, let’s keep demanding higher quality work from filmmakers—this way the future stories being told would not only entertain but also challenge us intellectually. texts.full_text: of circulating EBV-miRNAs as biomarkers for NNKTL. Sera of patients with NNKTL were subjected to miRNA polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-array analysis, after which serum EBV-miRNA levels were verified using quantitative PCR. The latter analysis revealed high miR-BART2-5p, miR-BART7-3p, miR-BART13-3p, and miR-BART1-5p expression levels in sera of patients with NNKTL and indicated accessive correlation between these EBV-miRNAs' serum expressions and patient prognosis. These findings suggest that circulating EBV-miRNA signatures might serve as potential biomarkers for monitoring the progression, managing therapy responses, or even early diagnosis of NNKTL. To validate our results further and explore their clinical implications more deeply, future studies are warranted to confirm these correlations in larger cohorts. Additionally, examining whether circulating EBV-miRNAs can distinguish between different stages of disease could be valuable for stratifying patients into appropriate treatment groups or predicting patient outcomes with greater precision. Understanding the molecular mechanisms behind how EBV-encoded miRNA influence NNKTL development and progression might also provide insights into novel therapeutic strategies. Targeting these viral micro-RNAs using anti-miR agents, for instance, could represent a new avenue of therapy against this aggressive lymphoma subtype. Moreover, incorporating circulating EBV-miRNAs as biomarkers in clinical practice may require further optimization to improve sensitivity and specificity given the heterogeneity within NNKTL. Standardizing these assays across different laboratories is essential for ensuring reliable results that can be translated into meaningful patient care improvements. In conclusion, our study highlights circulating EBV-miRNAs as promising biomarkers in managing nasal natural killer/T-cell lymphoma and suggests their potential utility not only in monitoring disease progression but also aiding early diagnosis. texts.full_text: The primary goal behind creating an isocentric treatment system for patients undergoing radiotherapy was the recognition that maintaining these individuals in upright positions could enhance their comfort and potentially improve therapeutic outcomes. By allowing better alignment of targeted areas with respect to radiation sources during both simulation phases (for planning purposes) as well post-treatment delivery, this chair design has been crafted specifically considering patient ergonomics. A key feature distinguishing the new isocentric treatment chairs from those previously available on market lies in their compatibility and versatility. The authors have designed a system that can be seamlessly integrated with existing radiotherapy simulators along side traditional therapy units - an aspect which ensures backward-compatibility without necessitating extensive modifications to current infrastructures. Moreover, this chair facilitates the implementation of isocentric setups during treatment planning stages as well post-implementation phases. This means patients are able either maintain a standard source-to-axis distance (SAD) for their machine or alternatively extend it further away from body structures - depending on what would be most beneficial to them based upon clinical assessments. Also: the effectof societal values on what constitutes a"disorder," particularly in cases where behavior patterns that might be considereddeviant or unacceptableby oneculture are accepted as normal within another; and lastly, the impact of individual differences—such as temperament, life experiences,and cultural background—on how personality disordersmanifest themselves. Personality disordershave long been recognized not onlyfor their debilitating effects on individuals but also because they challenge ourunderstanding of what constitutes mental illness. Unlike medical conditions where symptomsare often clearly defined by physical abnormalities or biochemical imbalances that can be measured through laboratory tests, the diagnosis and classification ofpersonality disorderslargely rely on observations made during clinical interviews with patients aswellas reports fromtheir family members and close friends. This relianceon subjective judgmenthas led to much debateabout whether certain behaviors should indeedclassify under a "disorder"category. For instance, what one culture may deem socially inappropriate or dysfunctional couldbe vieweddifferently in another societywhere such behavior might not have any significant impact on the individual'slife functioningsor relationships. Moreover, personality disorders can often present themselves as complex andmultifaceted conditionsthat do not neatlyfit intodistinctcategories.Many individuals with a diagnoseddisorder maydisplay symptoms of multiple types within this broadcategory—often blurring the lines between one type of condition to another. The forthcoming DSM-V aims at addressingsome shortcomings fromits predecessor, including clearer criteria for diagnosing personality disorders andpotentially reducingoverlaps that lead toinconsistent classification. Yetit is reasonable to expectnew challenges as wellwitheach update in diagnostic guidelines—the complexity inherent within the field of mentalhealth ensuresno single document canever be comprehensiveor universally accepted. The concept of'disorder'itself, particularly when applied to personality traits, continues to invite scrutiny and criticism from differentquartersincluding psychologists, psychiatrists, ethicists as wellas members ofaffected communities. texts.preview_tail: nosis. Conclusion DECT has emerged as a transformative tool in gout management, bridging the gap between clinical suspicion and definitive diagnosis. Its ability to visualize urate crystals with high
texts.full_text: Non-Invasive Diagnosis: DECT can detect urate crystals inaffectedjoints and soft tissues without the need for synovial fluid aspiration. This is critical in patients with atypicalpresentations or those where aspiration is inconclusive. 2. Specificity for Urate Deposits: By leveraging energy-dependent attenuation differences, DECT selectively highlights urate crystals (appearing in red in color-coded images) while distinguishing them from calcium-based deposits, which is vital for differentiating gout from other crystal-induced arthropathies. 3. SubclinicalTophus Detection: DECT identifiesurate deposits even in the absence of acute inflammation or clinical symptoms, enabling preemptive management and monitoring of disease progression. 4. Improved Treatment Monitoring:Serial DECT scans can assess the response to urate-lowering therapies (e.g., allopurinol) by quantifying the reduction in urate crystal burden over time. 5. Clinical Decision Support:In challenging cases (e.g., pseudogout, rheumatoidarthritis), DECT helps confirm or rule out gout, avoiding unnecessary invasive procedures andguiding targeted therapy. Comparative Advantages - vs.Ultrasound: DECT offers superior sensitivity for urate detection, especially in deep joints and complex anatomical regions (e.g., spine, sacroiliacjoints), where ultrasound may be limited. - vs. Conventional CT: Standard CT cannot differentiate urate from calciumcrystals, leading to diagnostic uncertainty. - vs. MRI:While MRI is excellent for soft tissue and inflammation, it lacksspecificity forurate crystals, requiring additional sequences or contrast agents. Limitations and Considerations - DECT is notyet widely available in all clinical settings due to cost and technical requirements. - Radiation exposure is slightly higher than ultrasound or MRI, though lower than conventional CT. - Expertise ininterpreting DECT images is essentialto avoid misdiagnosis. texts.full_text: well-known host of No Man's Land on CIUT 89.5 FM, along with popular Toronto DJ Fab, were dedicated to the task of keeping people pumped, providing excellent music at the beginning, middle and end of the evening. As usual, Howard Laurie skilfully guided Lula Lounge's stellar sound system and the rest of the staff, as always, kept the mood light. Juan Carlos Valencia Juan dedicated a song, "Estoy Contigo", meaning "I'm With You", to his father-in-law (who recently passed away) and to his wife and her family. This song filled the room with cool effects compliments of the keys and a heart-stirringly haunting saxophone solo. Oliver Miguel, on soprano sax was 'adopted' into the family for the next song, Iraq. He skillfully incorporated slow-paced klezmer strains that smoothly shifted into spinning top madness turning slowly back into a solo sax accompanied by smooth work on the keys. The resulting effect? The crowd went crazy! There was such a variety of rhythms and gyrations emanating from the stage during the evening that it was easy to think that the 'C' in Plan C stood for 'circus' as there was so much happening. At one point, the band was providing snake charmer grooves while Valencia skilfully and playfully guided the crowd through the following call-and-response: "When I say 'a', you say 'b'" and eventually, "When I say 'plan'", the crowd replyed with (you guessed it!) 'c'. It was cute, predictable, and elicited the expected laughter from the crowd. Later in the evening, a gaita (a Colombian instrument in the flute family) was produced. texts.full_text: ules in this section address the selection of steels for different steel markets – such as automotive, construction, engineering and offshore applications. Acting as the materials advisor in a multidisciplinary team, the user aims to select suitable steel in order to satisfy design specifications for a particular application. The major engineering design equations are studied during this process in order to select and compare the performance of steel and other metals and materials. Steel processing This section contains a series of interactive simulations of major steelmaking processes from raw materials, Basic oxygen steelmaking (BOS) and Electric arc furnace (EAF) steelmaking, secondary steelmaking, Continuous casting and Hot rolling. Different steel grades can be processed using two levels of operation which are suitable for students and graduates in the industry. The ‘student level’ provides a basic functionality enabling the user to understand and control the process principles without too many operational complications, whereas the ‘industry level’ has advanced functionality and the user needs to deal with practical disturbances that are not anticipated in advance. Real-time feedback is given on the composition and quality of the steel, on process time and temperature and incurred costs, to allow the process to be optimized. A user guide and more conventional supporting e-learning units are also available within each module to enable the learner to better understand the reactions. For example, in the secondary steelmaking module, there are sections on deoxidation, desulphurization, decarburization and dehydrogenation. Ferrous metallurgy The section on ferrous metallurgy contains modules on thermodynamics and kinetics, including strengthening mechanisms, phase transformation, recrystallisation and grain growth, heat treatment and steel properties. An introduction is provided on steel specifications, with exercises to ensure that they can be accurately interpreted. The learner can check mechanical properties of a given grade of steel by conducting virtual tensile, Charpy impact and hardness tests as well. Sustainability This section introduces the environmental aspects of steelmaking, usage and recycling processes. The learner gets a basic idea of Life Cycle Thinking

texts.full_text: with greater than 1 layer of granulosa cells, and in the number of follicof preantral antra. Furthermore, MSG-treated rats showed reduced numbers of primordial to primary stage ovarian follicle transitions on Days 7-23 compared to controls. These findings suggest that neonatal administration of monosodium L-glutamate (MSG) effectively blocks the selective increase in serum FSH concentration observed during normal rat development without significantly altering basal levels or affecting LH concentrations. This blockade results in impaired early stages of ovarian follicular growth and maturation, indicating a critical role for this transient rise in circulating FSH on prepubertal gonadal function. The study highlights how neonatal dietary factors can influence the endocrine system's regulation over reproductive development through mechanisms that involve modulation by hypothalamus-pituitary-gonad axis hormones. Such models may provide insights into human conditions where similar disruptions occur, potentially affecting fertility outcomes later in life due to altered early ovarian follicle recruitment and growth patterns. Fragile X syndrome is the most prevalent cause of inherited mental retardation and occurs in 2-6% of both females and males. Chromosomes and fragile X syndrome should be routinely investigated in patients with mental retardation of unknown aetiology. Molecular cytogenetics (FISH) provide specific diagnosis of several rare and clinically recognizable syndromes. Provision of extended clinical information is critical at referral, so that the most relevant investigations may be selected. Biochemical and molecular investigations can be performed for aneven larger number but are not routinely available because these methods require trained staff or highly specialized laboratories. texts.full_text: nload Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | Android | Email | RSS | More We’re at the end. Today is chapter 11, The Power Of Peer Advantage. Over the last 10 episodes of the podcast we’ve been taking a look – chapter-by-chapter – at the book, THE POWER OF PEERS by Leon Shapiro and Leo Bottary. This final chapter ties it all together and discusses the power we can access when we join forces with others, namely with peers. The chapter opens with the story of Don Guild, a pharmacist in Southern California who opened a small drugstore with his dad in 1961. Don opened a new store every year until he had 13 locations. In 1969 he joined a CEO peer advisory group where all the members were working to grow their own businesses. Don had historically struggled because he was trained as a pharmacist, but he hated being a pharmacist. By 1982 CVS was descending on Southern California. That prompted Don to think it might be time to get out. A year later CVS continued to expand, but Don was able to use the knowledge and support from his peer group to adapt and transform his business. The group, which Don was a part of, was the first of many peer group successes. texts.full_text: My Whiffs of Home Have you ever been walking to a destination and you suddenly smell home? It just floats right by you and then it’s gone. And you inhale deeply, willing the scent to come back and flood your nostrils one more time with a memory once lived. The scent is unique to your senses and the environment you lived in. For me, I sometimes catch my father’s cologne, the stale kind that has settled into the fabric of his shirt after a full work day of sweating. Other times it is the smokey remnants of suya rubbed with peanut spice and mixed with chopped onions and their purple hue. Sometimes it is the stench of decay, a reminder of the piles upon piles of rubbish strangely colorful on the side of my streets. But I inhale it all. texts.full_text: nuclear protein ([@b1-mmr-19-05-3685]) that regulates transforming growth factor-β (TGF-β)/Smad signaling. It is part of the intermediary factor 1 family, which includes four identified Tif1 members in mammals (α to δ), with modulatory roles in the innate immune response and inflammation processes. Tif1γ was initially described a decade ago ([@b2-mmr-19-05-3685]); however, this protein has recently gained increasing interest in oncology due to its function as an oncogene and a tumor suppressor gene ([@b3-mmr-19-05-3685],[@b4-mmr-19-05-3685]). Similar to its homolog transcriptional cofactors (Tif1α and Tif1β), Tif1γ contains multiple domains, including the RING finger, B boxes, coiled coil and a PHD-bromodomain that stabilizes Tif1γ chromatin occupancy and enhances its E3 ligase activity ([@b5-mmr-19-05-3685]--[@b7-mmr-19-05-3685]). Despite its structural similarities, Tif1γ exhibits distinct functional characteristics that set it apart from Tif1 family members. For Tif1γ's ability to regulate the TGF-β/Smad signaling pathway is crucial for the control of cell growth, differentiation, and apoptosis. This regulation can be either stimulatory or inhibitory, depending on the cellular context and the presence of specific binding partners or modifications. Recent studies have highlighted the importance of Tif1γ in various physiological and pathological conditions. In the context of cancer, Tif1γ has been shown to play a role in the development and progression of several types of malignancies, including breast, lung, and liver cancers. Its function can vary from promoting tumorigenesis by enhancing cell proliferation and survival to suppressing tumor growth by inducing cell cycle arrest and apoptosis. The dual nature of Tif1γ as both an oncogene and a tumor suppressor is a topic of intense research, as understanding the mechanisms that govern its activity could provide new therapeutic targets for cancer treatment. As much as people hope their business contract is not a big deal or something that will never happen, the reality of this situation can be devastating for any small to medium-sized business. In today's fast-paced digital world, IT emergencies are inevitable and unavoidable at times. When an IT emergency strikes your business, it could lead to data loss, system downtime, compromised security systems or even worst-case scenarios like ransomware attacks which can cripple operations for days on end if not handled promptly. That's where a professional help desk team comes in handy! A dedicated and experienced IT support service will have the tools, expertise, and resources necessary to quickly identify problems, implement solutions, and restore your business systems back online as swiftly as possible. A good IT support company understands that every minute of downtime is critical for businesses. They are trained professionals who can handle a wide range of issues from hardware failures or software bugs to more complex cybersecurity threats like phishing attacks or malware infections. So how do you know if your business needs professional help desk services? Here's what you should consider: 1. System Complexity: If your IT infrastructure is highly complex with numerous systems, servers and devices that need regular maintenance. 2. Staff Expertise: If the in-house staff lacks specialized skills or experience to handle critical issues on their own without external assistance.

Finally, we show two numerical examples to illustrate the effectiveness of th--- 1. Introduction Synchronization of harmonic oscillators is a fundamental problem in fields such as physics, engineering, and biology. In many practical scenarios, the communication between oscillators is constrained by limited bandwidth and precision, necessitating the use of quantized data. This technical note addresses the synchronization problem for a set of networked harmonic oscillators using quantized sampled velocity data. 2. Quantized Coupling Protocol We propose a coupling protocol where the velocities of the oscillators are encoded using a uniform quantizer with a zooming parameter. The quantizer maps the continuous velocity data to a discrete set of values, reducing the amount of information that needs to be transmitted across the network. The zooming parameter can be either fixed or adjustable, allowing for flexibility in the design of the protocol. 3. Synchronization with Fixed Zooming Parameter We establish sufficient conditions under which the networked harmonic oscillators converge to a bounded neighborhood of the synchronized orbits when using a fixed zooming parameter. Specifically, we show that if the coupling strength and the quantization level satisfy certain inequalities, then the oscillators will synchronize up to a error bound determined by the quantization resolution. 4. Synchronization with Zooming Parameter To achieve exact synchronization, we design an adjustable zooming parameter that dynamically adapts based on the state of the oscillators. By carefully tuning the zooming parameter, we can ensure that the quantized coupling protocol drives the oscillators to synchronize precisely, overcoming the limitations imposed by the fixed quantization levels. 5.
